source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Review + Comments

quotes.quote: Those inconsistencies will likely translate to inconsistent results on the course.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3

quotes.quote: The compression consistency across the sample was rated as Poor with a 15-point compression range across the sample, leading to eight percent being flagged as Bad.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3

quotes.quote: It is almost as if no two balls are the same and thereby potentially affecting your performance on the course.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3

quotes.quote: I've been playing the V3 balls for a while and they are fine for me. As a high handicapper my swing is wildly more inconsistent than the slight differences in balls that MGS finds.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: I tried V2 and they were really spinny. Great around the greens but sucked distance. I stopped playing them though because of wear issues. They scuffed up after just a few holes.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: I use the Kirkland v3 for hitting into my practice net at home. They're very poor quality golf balls. Cover durability is awful. They breakdown after a few wedge shots. Wouldn't recommend gaming these.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: Hate to say it but they're not worth the price.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: In the end. You usually get what you pay for.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: A 15 Delta difference in compression is huge to me, given that I have a moderate swing speed. One ball will feel like a rock & next will feel much softer. I don't want that inconsistency.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: I really liked the 4 piece Kirkland and this article confirmed my suspicions that the new ball is inferior and feels hard as a rock to me.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: I use kirkland golf balls. I believe them to be just fine for my swing speed and game. Won 1st place in league with the kirkland golf ball.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: I have the yellow ones and they seem to scuff up badly after only a few hits. I feel my swing is pretty consistent and every ball I hit seems to fly differently, almost like they are not balanced well.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: I do not think I will be wasting my money on them anymore.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: Really simply it means that one ball is going to perform differently than the next. They'll fly differently, spin differently, and ultimately travel different distances.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments (Tony Covey)

quotes.quote: It's a bit like mixing different models into the same dozen or a bit like if I randomly bent that 9-iron you mentioned a degree or two in either direction every time you pulled it out of the bag.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments (Tony Covey)

quotes.quote: V3 suits my game. I'm a high handicapper usually 2-3 strokes on the greens. Distance is not a factor. Consistent yardage is. V3 is a short game ball with great spin. Won't change.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: The only good ball Kirkland produced was the first version, the model they had to stop making & selling due to Acushnet Company (Titleist) lawsuit for patent infringement.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: Titleist pays insane money to tour pros and other endorsements. All factored into their price.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: If the inconsistency of the golf balls you play can take off or add another 5 yards, it could increase your dispersion by another 50%. Definitely not going to help your cause.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments

quotes.quote: Compression can be surprisingly difficult to nail consistently. It requires precise mixing of the ingredients, and if the raw materials aren't stored properly things can go to hell pretty quickly.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments (Tony Covey)

quotes.quote: Some lower-tier factories have been known to store their raw materials outdoors.
quotes.source: MyGolfSpy Ball Lab - Kirkland V3 Comments (Tony Covey)

quotes.quote: I bought a variety of balls off of lostgolfballs.com to see which one I liked best.
quotes.source: Reddit r/golf

quotes.quote: I switched to the Maxfli Tour X for a good portion of this season. I have found the cheaper balls get shredded compared to the ProV1.
quotes.source: Reddit r/golf - Pro V1 alternatives

quotes.quote: Played PRO V for the last 10 years...switched to Vice PRO Plus...amazing feel..great durability and 30 Canadian dollars cheaper.
quotes.source: Facebook Golf Groups

quotes.quote: The idea that only high spin single digit players need a premium golf ball is a waste of money for most who aren't a single digit handicap.
quotes.source: GolfWRX Forums

quotes.quote: In the end, if you can't quantify a difference in anything but feel, it's not worth changing.
quotes.source: Facebook Golf Groups

quotes.quote: Unless you are getting paid to play golf, a premium golf ball will not make a difference. For most golfers it's a waste of money.
quotes.source: Quora

quotes.quote: Not worth playing with it if there's money on the line.
quotes.source: Reddit r/golf - premium balls thread

quotes.quote: You don't need to play a premium golf ball.
quotes.source: Reddit r/golf

quotes.quote: Mike Cohen must have got a dodgy batch?? Never found them to be inconsistent.
quotes.source: Facebook MyGolfSpy
